## [00:00 - 03:05]

So when you first launch you're going off preconceived ideas you have about your product and what your customers want to hear, but now that you have customers you're in this awesome position where you can ask those customers what it is that they actually liked about your product and what it is that actually made them buy. So we did this by asking our customers to fill out a survey and the kind of responses that we got back are really insightful but you have to ask the right questions. So the kind of questions you want to ask are questions that will help you better market to your customers. So as you can see here on the survey that we did in Survey Monkey we said what kind of riding did you first purchase Quadlock 4? Is it commuting, bikepacking, indoor training, road cycling, motorcycling, mountain biking, touring? By understanding the product mix there or the user mix we could then tailor our content because we find out most people are road cyclists then they're mountain bikers and then they're commuters and then the other ones are quite small. So now when you see our content you'll notice that it's more road cyclists then some mountain bikers and then some commuters because we know that's how people want to be communicated to or we'll have just specific content for road cyclists, just specific content for commuters and specific mountain biking content. The other things we asked is what's your favourite cycling app? Just because then we know when we show photos we can show photos of the phone with their favourite apps on board. Some other things we asked is what are the three product features that were most important to you at the time of purchasing? And this was huge for us because we learnt that one of the main points we were pushing was not important to anyone. So when we took that point away which was the lightweight point, we took that away, we took it out of our copy, we didn't really talk about it much anymore and we then put back in protective impact resistant case. We put it into all our videos, we put it in some of our copy, we really highlighted that fact because for most people that was a really important point that we just completely missed. So now what you need to do is think to yourself what is it that we don't know about our customers, what assumptions have we been making up till now, what's an unbiased way we can ask our customers these questions to get back really usable information that will lead us to writing better copy, better call to actions, making better videos and taking better photos because the closer you can get to telling a customer what they want to hear, the cheaper you'll be able to acquire that customer. So it's really important. Another thing to keep in mind is that you want to get really good quality information back from your customers and you want to get as many of them as you can to fill this out but you don't want to burden them too much. So the way we've found success is firstly I wouldn't offer a reward or a prize or something

## [03:05 - 05:46]

for filling it out because you don't want people just to go through and give you garbage information, you want good information, you want customers that care to bother filling it out because they want to help you. So a good tip here is we actually did a little emoji of the please help us and it was sort of appealing to the good night's side of our customers and they were more than happy to help us. I think we had over 15,000 people come back and complete this survey which made the data invaluable. The next thing is when sending it out, give people a time expectation. So if the survey is going to take two minutes, say two minutes, it's going to take five minutes, say five minutes. You don't want people to rush through the end and give you garbage because if you get garbage and you think it's real and you start tailoring your content to the garbage that people are giving you, you're not going to get the desired results. So yeah, really make sure that you're getting good, clean data and when you're asking those questions make sure that the information you get back is going to be usable. So surveys like this are not something you send out every week. This is a big body of work that you sit down, you craft very carefully and you send it in one big hit, you get back all this data, then you crunch all that data, you look for the useful parts and once you've got it, then you start making decisions and working back to that data. But it should be the kind of questions that are not going to change month on month. So if you do this one year, you should be able to use it like the next year because once you have a lot of people come back and tell you the same thing, you can get a whole heap more people, they'll probably tell you the same thing at scale. So that's the cool thing about doing something like this at scale. If you're asking 10 of your friends, you can get 10 different answers. If you ask 15,000 people, well if you get 15,000 results or even two or three thousand results depending on the scale of your business, you're going to get back information that quite clearly shows you this is important to the biggest majority of the people and that's what you've got to do. You've got to tailor your content to the biggest majority of your customers. So to get people to fill out the survey, you need to send out a link. We did it mainly via email, you can do it via social media and I think it's important to send it to both your customers and your non-customers that you may have in your database because there's also a lot of good information you can get back from your non-customers as well as your customers. If people haven't purchased, why haven't they purchased? So in this database we did, if they were a customer, they got these questions, if they are a non-customer, they got these questions. And you can tailor all of that through a product like SurveyMonkey. So as an action item, what you need to do is think about some questions you could ask your customers that will give you the information to better sell to them, write them down in the workbook provided and I'll see you in the next lesson.
